overview: Lorwyn Eclipsed is a slow, boardstall-heavy format built around five main typal archetypes and the Vivid multi-color mechanic. Success depends on identifying your lane early to maximize typal payoffs.

mechanics.name: Vivid
mechanics.description: Cards that scale in power based on the number of colors of mana spent to cast them (X).
mechanics.draft_tip: Vivid cards like Explosive Prodigy are solid even with X=2, but excel in Green-based ramp decks.

mechanics.name: Blight
mechanics.description: A mechanic focusing on -1/-1 counters to punish or weaken opponents.
mechanics.draft_tip: Blight works best as a support mechanic in Goblins or Midrange shells rather than a standalone build-around.

mechanics.name: Convoke
mechanics.description: Tap creatures to help pay for spells. Centered in Merfolk (WU).
mechanics.draft_tip: Highly effective with vigilance creatures and go-wide strategies.

mechanics.name: Eclipsed
mechanics.description: A cycle of hybrid-mana creatures that dig for cards of their type or specific lands.
mechanics.draft_tip: These are premium uncommons. Take them early to solidify your position in an archetype.

archetypes.name: Elves / Graveyard
archetypes.description: The best deck in the format. Uses mill and recursion to outvalue opponents.
archetypes.strategy: Use self-mill to enable Morcant's Eyes and Gloom Ripper while overwhelming with Elf tokens.

archetypes.name: Kithkin / Aggro
archetypes.description: Straightforward aggressive deck with strong board presence.
archetypes.strategy: Curve out early and use anthems or combat tricks to push through damage.

archetypes.name: Merfolk / Convoke
archetypes.description: Go-wide tempo deck using Convoke and tap-synergies.
archetypes.strategy: Develop a board of Merfolk to cast expensive Convoke spells like Temporal Cleansing for cheap.

archetypes.name: Elementals / Big Spells
archetypes.description: Late-game deck that rewards casting high-mana spells and splashing colors.
archetypes.strategy: Use Evoke for early value and stabilize for a massive late-game finish with Elementals.

archetypes.name: Vivid / Ramp
archetypes.description: 5-color control deck utilizing the best cards in the draft via fixing.
archetypes.strategy: Prioritize Shimmerwilds Growth and Noggle Robber to enable your high-impact Vivid payoffs.

archetypes.name: Goblins / Blight
archetypes.description: Aggressive deck using -1/-1 counters and Goblin synergies.
archetypes.strategy: Look for Eclipsed Boggart and Boggart Mischief to keep the pressure high.

general_tips: Lorwyn Eclipsed is slower than recent sets; don't be afraid to keep a slower hand with high-impact late-game cards.
The Eclipsed cycle of uncommons are effectively mythic uncommons; they are almost always worth P1P1.
Elves (BG) is the deepest and most powerful archetype, but it is often heavily contested.
